content.problemStatement:
You want to rent a movie. There are NNN movies and the iii-th movie has an IMDB score of AiA_iAi​ and costs BiB_iBi​ to rent. Note that IMDB scores can only be **integers** in the range 111 to 101010 for the sake of this problem.

You will only watch movies with an IMDB score of at least 777. Find the minimum cost of a movie that you are willing to watch.

There may be no movies available with an IMDB score of at least 777. Print −1-1−1 then.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the number of movies.
The iii-th of the next NNN lines contain 222 integers each - AiA_iAi​ and BiB_iBi​.




Output Format
For each test case, output the minimum cost of a movie with a IMDB rating of at least 777, or −1-1−1 if no such movie.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : You can buy the 2nd2^{nd}2nd movie which has an IMDB rating of 777 and a cost of 350350350. The 1st1^{st}1st movie has a larger IMDB rating, but is also more costly. the 3rd3^{rd}3rd movie has lower than 777 IMDB rating.
Test Case 2 : There are no movies available with IMDB rating of at least 777.
